candidates.comparison: compare_two_settings: exact text and dict equality between vLLM configs
candidates.oracle: vLLM baseline vs vLLM with cpu-offload-gb=1, same engine different config
candidates.helper: compare_two_settings (tests/utils.py:1165) -> compare_all_settings -> exact == on result dicts
candidates.fixtures: none (uses compare_two_settings which launches its own servers)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES — exact text equality between two runs
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: Unlikely — same engine, same weights, same model; offload just moves weights CPU<->GPU
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: NO — strong contract: CPU offload/reload restoration must produce identical output. Same engine, same seed, same config except offloading. The contract is that weight offloading is lossless.

candidates.comparison: torch.allclose(output, torch.ones_like(output) * 3) and memory size comparison
candidates.oracle: Deterministic integer arithmetic (0+1+2=3) with tolerance-based comparison
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: NO — uses torch.allclose (tolerance-based), and values are deterministic integer operations (zero_() + 1 + 2 = 3)
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: NO — integer-like floating point arithmetic (adding small integers to zeroed tensors) is exact and unaffected by numeric changes
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: NO — strong contract: custom memory pool tensors must be usable with default pool tensors; sleep/wake must restore values. Deterministic arithmetic guarantees result.

candidates.oracle: CUDA graph replay fidelity — identity matmul (eye) + add 1, deterministic ops
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: NO — uses torch.allclose (tolerance-based), operations are deterministic (matmul with identity matrix, +1)
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: NO — x @ eye(1024) = x exactly, x+1 is exact; these are not subject to numeric drift
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: NO — strong contract: CUDA graph replay in same execution family must produce identical results. Weight restoration after sleep/wake is a fidelity guarantee.

candidates.oracle: Sleep/wake restoration fidelity — same engine, same prompt, same params, before vs after sleep+wake
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES — exact string equality on generated text
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: Unlikely — same engine instance, weights restored from checkpoint; sleep/wake is a save/restore operation
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: NO — strong contract: sleep+wake restoration must reproduce identical output. Same engine, same weights (restored from safetensors/checkpoint), same greedy sampling (temperature=0). The contract is weight restoration fidelity.

candidates.oracle: Deep sleep (level=2) restoration fidelity — weights reloaded from disk, compare output before/after
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES — exact string equality on generated text
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: Unlikely — same engine, weights reloaded from same checkpoint file; reload_weights is a deserialization operation
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: NO — strong contract: deep sleep + wake + reload_weights must reproduce identical output. Same engine, same model weights reloaded from disk, same greedy sampling.

candidates.oracle: Async deep sleep restoration fidelity — same as test_deep_sleep but via AsyncLLMEngine
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES — exact string equality on generated text
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: Unlikely — same engine (async variant), weights reloaded from same checkpoint
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: NO — strong contract: async deep sleep + wake + reload_weights must reproduce identical output. Same engine, same weights, same greedy sampling.

candidates.oracle: Deep sleep with FP8 KV cache restoration fidelity — compare output before/after sleep+wake
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES — exact string equality on generated text
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: Unlikely — same engine, same weights restored, FP8 KV cache is a storage optimization that should not affect restored weight values
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: NO — strong contract: deep sleep + wake + reload_weights with FP8 KV cache must reproduce identical output. Same engine, same checkpoint, same greedy sampling.

candidates.helper: compare_two_settings (tests/utils.py:1165) -> compare_all_settings -> exact == on result dicts
candidates.fixtures: none (uses compare_two_settings which launches its own servers)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: YES — exact text equality between two server runs
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: Unlikely — same engine, same model weights; prefetch offloading only changes when weights are moved to GPU, not their values
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: NO — strong contract: prefetch offloading is a weight scheduling optimization that must not change model outputs. Same engine, same weights, same seed. The contract is that offload/prefetch is transparent.
